title: 10 best herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy are plant-based remedies that help alleviate symptoms of an enlarged prostate. They work by reducing inflammation and improving urinary flow. The best herbal essential oils for this condition include Serenoa repens, Urtica dioica, Curcuma longa, Pygeum africanum, Ginkgo biloba, Prunus africana, Zingiber officinale, Cucurbita pepo, Sabal serrulata, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, benign prostatic hypertrophy can lead to urinary incontinence, painful urination, and even kidney damage, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it targets the key underlying issue of DHT conversion, which is a primary contributor to enlarged prostate tissue. The oil's active ingredients, specifically beta-carotene and fatty acids, have been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT). By reducing DHT levels, saw palmetto essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of BPH such as frequent urination, weak stream, and prostate discomfort.
remedy_properties: 5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's fatty acid and sterol constituents can inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH symptoms, thus reducing prostate size and alleviating urinary tract issues.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The extract may help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which is often associated with BPH, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids and enzymes.

Androgen receptor modulation: Saw palmetto's constituents may modulate androgen receptors, potentially reducing the binding of DHT to these receptors and thereby decreasing the stimulation of prostate growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in fatty acids and phytosterols, which help to regulate hormone imbalances and reduce inflammation associated with BPH.
Fruits: The fruits contain beta-sitosterol, a natural compound that has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which is linked to BPH.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh serenoa repens leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried off for better oil yield.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation at room temperature or by spreading thin layers on paper bags for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for steam distillation using a mortar and pestle.
Perform steam distillation of the crushed plant material with 1:2 ratio of plant material to water at 100°c for 30-40 minutes.
Collect the extracted essential oil in a clean glass container and store it in an airtight bottle in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: African plum essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as beta-sitosterol and lupeol, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms like urinary frequency and nocturia associated with BPH. Additionally, the essential oil's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue helps to ease bladder obstruction and improve urine flow, providing relief for men suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Pygeum's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as isoflavones and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which is a common characteristic of BPH.
Alpha-blocker activity: The bark of Pygeum africanum contains compounds that have been shown to relax smooth muscle in the prostate and urethra, improving urine flow and reducing symptoms of BPH.

Antioxidant activity: Pygeum's antioxidant properties may help protect the prostate gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of P. africana contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat urinary tract problems and prostatic issues, including BPH.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of P. africana are rich in phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them suitable for treating BPH symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of prunus africana bark in the morning after it has been dried for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried prunus africana bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for approximately 15 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered prunus africana bark with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a dark place at room temperature for 14 days, shaking it gently once every day to facilitate the extraction process.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the infused carrier oil to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease urinary symptoms associated with BPH. The oil's ability to relax muscles and calm the prostate gland may also help alleviate urinary frequency, nocturia, and incomplete bladder emptying. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant properties may help protect against cellular damage and promote overall prostate health, making it a potentially effective natural remedy for managing BPH symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of BPH such as urinary frequency and urgency.

Alpha-blocker activity: The oil's alpha-blocking compounds help relax smooth muscle tissues in the prostate gland, allowing for improved urine flow and reducing symptoms of BPH, such as hesitancy and weak stream.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging: Stinging nettle essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the prostate gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are ideal for producing essential oils that help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Seeds: Seeds contain a high concentration of fatty acids and antioxidants, making them an excellent choice for creating therapeutic essential oils that support prostate health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves by cutting them from the plant in early morning dew for optimal potency retention.
Dry the harvested nettle leaves using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours or until crumbly texture is achieved.
Steam distill the dried nettle leaves with distilled water at a ratio of 1:5 for 4 hours to extract essential oil.
Separate and filter the extracted essential oil from the distillation condensate using a coffee filter paper and store in dark glass bottles.
Combine the filtered essential oil with jojoba oil at a ratio of 10% nettle oil to 90% jojoba oil, shaking well before storing in refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and shrink enlarged prostate tissue. By reducing inflammation and blocking DHT production, these oils may help alleviate symptoms of BPH, including frequent urination, weak urine flow, and bladder pressure.
remedy_properties: Saw Palmetto: Inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH growth, thereby reducing prostate enlargement and improving symptoms such as frequent urination.
Pygeum Africanum: Reduces inflammation and relaxes smooth muscle tissue in the prostate gland, which helps alleviate symptoms like urinary frequency and urgency, as well as pain during urination.

Beta-Sitosterol (found in Saw Palmetto, Pygeum Africanum, and other herbs): Has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce prostate swelling and improve urine flow by relaxing the muscles surrounding the urethra.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea plant contain a rich source of flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Barks: Tea bark contains phenolic compounds that have been demonstrated to exhibit alpha-reducing activity, which helps to reduce the production of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of camellia sinensis and wash them gently in cold running water for 30 seconds.
Steam distill the washed camellia sinensis leaves at 100°c for 3 hours to obtain essential oil.
Combine distilled essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3 drops per ml.
Store the prepared herbal essential oil in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.
Apply the prepared herbal essential oil topically three times a day, massaging gently into prostate area for 2 minutes each application.

alpha-blocker-like properties: Silver birch bark extract has been found to exhibit a mechanism similar to alpha-blockers, which are medications used to treat BPH symptoms by relaxing smooth muscle in the prostate gland and bladder neck.

remedy_recipe: Harvest freshly grown betula pendula leaves from a sustainable source.
Wash the harvested leaves with cold distilled water for thorough cleaning.
Dry the leaves in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a 1:5 ratio of leaf to water for 90 minutes.
Filter and store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and stems in the early morning when dew is still present on the plants weighing around 100 grams.
Steam distill the harvested plant material using a clevenger apparatus or a similar device for approximately 3-4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a glass container with a cap tightly sealed after separating it from the water and plant residue.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment for about 15 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark amber glass bottles with tight-fitting caps at room temperature away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains a unique combination of phytoestrogens that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decanting it off the top layer after cooling the mixture in a glass container.
Store the curcuma longa essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid and keep it refrigerated at a temperature below 10°c for up to 2 years.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy with herbal essential oils is by combining saw palmetto oil, pygeum oil, and beta-sitosterol oil. remedies.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it is rich in phytosterols and fatty acids, which may help reduce inflammation and promote a healthy prostate environment. The oil may also help to regulate hormonal balances and improve urinary function. remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: It helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy by inhibiting the growth of prostate cells and reducing prostate size.

Ursane triterpenes (including ursolic acid and urs-12-en-28-oic acid): They help to relax the muscles of the prostate and urinary tract, reducing the symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy such as difficulty urinating and increased frequency of urination.

Furostanol saponins: They help to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to the growth and enlargement of the prostate gland in benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most used part of Serenoa repens for making essential oils to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain a high concentration of saw palmetto's active compounds.
Leaves: Leaves are used in conjunction with roots to make essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy because they add a complementary set of bioactive compounds.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are sometimes used to make essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy because they retain some of the same bioactive compounds as the roots, but in smaller quantities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh serenoa repens roots in early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried roots in a 1:5 ratio of plant material to water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water distillate using a separatory funnel within 30 minutes.
Store the extracted serenoa repens essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 4 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pygeum africanum essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it is rich in lipophilic extracts that inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone known to stimulate prostate cell growth. remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kilograms of pygeum africanum bark from mature trees under the shade to prevent drying.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 500 grams of dried bark into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Steam distill 500 grams of powder in a distillation apparatus at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the extracted essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to obtain 50 milliliters of pure oil.

Store the pure ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms associated with an enlarged prostate. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, may also help to relax the muscles and improve blood flow to the prostate gland, promoting a reduction in inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's warming and soothing properties may provide relief from urinary frequency and urgency, improving overall prostate health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to decrease the size of the enlarged prostate and alleviate associated symptoms.

Zingerone: This constituent exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, thereby alleviating symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Buds: They are used because they contain essential oils with potential anti-androgenic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Roots: They are used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help in managing the symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with clean scissors to prevent contamination.
Steam distill the rhizomes at 100°c for 2 hours to separate oil from water.
Collect the distilled oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 4°c.
Test the oil for quality and purity using gas chromatography and spectrometry before use.

remedy_constituents: Sterols: Sterols have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the prostate gland associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have antioxidant properties, which may help protect the prostate gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially slowing down the progression of benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Lignans: Lignans have anti-androgenic properties, which may help reduce the levels of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to the growth of the prostate gland and the development of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Used in culinary and other applications.
Seeds: Used as a food source and in various products.
Leaves: Used for their nutritional value.
Leaves: Used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy due to their anti-androgenic properties.
Roots: Used to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy due to their anti-androgenic properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sabal serrulata essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains unique compounds that support prostate health. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the urinary tract. It also contains beta-sitosterol, a natural compound that may help to shrink enlarged prostate tissue. Additionally, the oil's diuretic properties can help to increase urine flow and alleviate symptoms associated with BPH, such as frequent urination and urinary retention. This natural remedy offers a promising solution for men seeking to manage BPH symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: These compounds have been shown to help reduce the size of the prostate gland, alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), and improve urinary flow.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and caffeic acid, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with BPH.

Flavonoids: Specifically, flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, have been demonstrated to possess anti-androgenic activity, which can help reduce the growth of the prostate gland and alleviate symptoms of BPH.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their saponin content, which has anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties that may help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Roots: They are used due to their saponin content, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Fruits: They are used due to their saponin content, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sabal serrulata leaves and stems in early morning to prevent oil degradation.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dry place for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material at a temperature of 100 to 110 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the herbal sabal serrulata essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds help to relax the muscles in the prostate gland and reduce swelling, alleviating discomfort and urinary frequency associated with BPH. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation also helps to promote a healthy prostate environment, allowing for the normal flow of urine and reducing the risk of complications such as urinary retention and infection. Regular use may also help to slow prostate growth.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Linalyl acetate: As an anti-inflammatory compound, it may help alleviate symptoms and slow down the growth of prostate tissue.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-androgenic properties, which may help reduce the growth of prostate tissue by inhibiting the production of androgens that contribute to benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Lavender leaves are often used because they contain compounds similar to those in the flowers, which can help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Stems: Lavender stems may be used due to their high content of sesquiterpene and monoterpene hydrocarbons, which can contribute to the oil's therapeutic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal oil content within 7-10 days.
Steam distill the harvested flowers with a 1:5 ratio of flowers to water at 80-100°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distillate and allow it to settle for 2 hours to separate the essential oil from the water.
Decant the essential oil from the water using a pipette or siphon and transfer it to a clean container.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 10-20°c.
